---
title: "S2S Lab 5"
site: bookdown::bookdown_site
output: bookdown::gitbook
documentclass: book
link-citations: yes
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, eval=TRUE, comment=NA)
library(webexercises)
library(PASWR2)
library(MASS)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)
```

# Welcome!

Welcome to the the fifth S2S lab. This lab moves away from teaching specific coding practise and starts to use what we have learned in previous labs to start exploring some ideas in statistics. Specifically, this lab will introduce the concept of sampling distributions - what they are, how they're constructed and how we can use R to help us in creating them.

The sampling distribution is also covered in [Chapter 6 Sampling and Sampling Distributions](https://read.kortext.com/reader/pdf/92741/351) of *Probability and Statistics with R*, specifically [Section 6.4 Sampling Distribution of $\bar X$](https://read.kortext.com/reader/pdf/92741/359) of textbook runs through a similar example that the majority of this lab uses.

## Investigating Engagement in Level 2 Statistics

You are invited to consent to your data to be included in an educational research study to evaluate student levels of engagement and teaching styles in Levels 1&2 Statistics courses in the School of Mathematics and Statistics.

To participate you only need to read the Participant Information Sheet and submit your name and student number.

Level 2 Stats lecturers and tutors will have no knowledge of who has/hasn't given consent for their data to be included in the study.

There is also the optional extra opportunity to join a focus group.

Please click on the [link to Moodle](https://moodle.gla.ac.uk/course/view.php?id=38599#section-7) to see all the information and give your consent.

[![Moodle](images/Moodle.svg){width=40%}](https://moodle.gla.ac.uk/course/view.php?id=38599#section-7)
